title: Exploring Dengue Data with BigDataPE
text: BigDataPE package provides simple secure interface accessing datasets published Big Data PE platform Government State Pernambuco, Brazil. vignette walk full package workflow using Dengue, Zika Chikungunya dataset — record notified cases health units (public private) Recife, shared State Health Department Pernambuco (Secretaria Estadual de Saude de Pernambuco). Access note: Although dataset public, must submit access request Big Data PE platform connected PE Conectado network VPN query data API. Without connection, requests time return service-unavailable error.

title: Exploring Dengue Data with BigDataPE
text: Load package helper libraries used analysis:
code: library(BigDataPE) library(dplyr) library(tibble)

title: Exploring Dengue Data with BigDataPE
text: first step store authentication token associated dataset. token provided Big Data PE platform access request approved. can verify token stored correctly:
code: bdpe_store_token("dengue", "your-token-here") #> ✔ Token stored in environment variable: `BigDataPE_dengue` bdpe_list_tokens() #> [1] "dengue"

title: Exploring Dengue Data with BigDataPE
text: bdpe_fetch_data() direct way query API. Let’s fetch first 100 records: dataset contains 126 columns detailed information notification, including demographics, symptoms, laboratory results, warning signs, severity indicators case outcome.

title: Exploring Dengue Data with BigDataPE
text: API supports additional filters query parameter. example, fetch notifications year 2020: can also filter municipality residence. IBGE code Recife 261160: Filters can combined. fetch female cases Recife:
code: dengue_2020 <- bdpe_fetch_data(   "dengue",   limit = 50,   offset = 0,   query = list(notificacao_ano = "2020") ) nrow(dengue_2020) #> [1] 50 dengue_recife <- bdpe_fetch_data(   "dengue",   limit = 100,   offset = 0,   query = list(co_municipio_residencia = "261160") ) nrow(dengue_recife) #> [1] 100 dengue_female_recife <- bdpe_fetch_data(   "dengue",   limit = 100,   offset = 0,   query = list(     co_municipio_residencia = "261160",     tp_sexo = "F"   ) ) nrow(dengue_female_recife)

title: Exploring Dengue Data with BigDataPE
text: data volume large, use bdpe_fetch_chunks() paginates automatically. fetch 1,009 records blocks 500:
code: all_data <- bdpe_fetch_chunks(   "dengue",   total_limit = Inf,   chunk_size = 500,   verbosity = 1 ) #> ℹ Fetched 500 records (total: 500). #> ℹ Fetched 500 records (total: 1000). #> ℹ Fetched 9 records (total: 1009). #> ✔ Fetching complete: 1009 records retrieved.  dim(all_data) #> [1] 1009  126

title: Exploring Dengue Data with BigDataPE
text: data hand can run quick exploratory summaries.

title: Exploring Dengue Data with BigDataPE
code: all_data |>   count(tp_sexo, sort = TRUE) #> # A tibble: 3 x 2 #>   tp_sexo     n #>   <chr>   <int> #> 1 F         561 #> 2 M         443 #> 3 I           5

title: Exploring Dengue Data with BigDataPE
code: all_data |>   count(notificacao_ano, sort = TRUE) #> # A tibble: 1 x 2 #>   notificacao_ano     n #>   <chr>           <int> #> 1 2020             1009

title: Exploring Dengue Data with BigDataPE
text: Symptom fields use "1" yes "2" . can compute proportion symptom:
code: symptoms <- c("febre", "mialgia", "cefaleia", "exantema", "vomito",               "nausea", "dor_costas", "conjutivite", "artrite",               "artralgia", "dor_retro")  all_data |>   summarise(across(all_of(symptoms), ~ mean(.x == "1", na.rm = TRUE))) |>   tidyr::pivot_longer(everything(),                       names_to  = "symptom",                       values_to = "proportion") |>   arrange(desc(proportion)) #> # A tibble: 11 x 2 #>    symptom      proportion #>    <chr>             <dbl> #>  1 febre             0.914 #>  2 cefaleia          0.531 #>  3 mialgia           0.512 #>  4 artralgia         0.194 #>  5 exantema          0.181 #>  6 dor_costas        0.155 #>  7 vomito            0.150 #>  8 nausea            0.139 #>  9 dor_retro         0.125 #> 10 conjutivite       0.053 #> 11 artrite           0.046

title: Exploring Dengue Data with BigDataPE
code: all_data |>   filter(no_bairro_residencia != "") |>   count(no_bairro_residencia, sort = TRUE) |>   head(10) #> # A tibble: 10 x 2 #>    no_bairro_residencia     n #>    <chr>                <int> #>  1 IBURA                  46 #>  2 VARZEA                 37 #>  3 COHAB                  33 #>  4 BOA VIAGEM             30 #>  5 AGUA FRIA              27 #>  ...

title: Exploring Dengue Data with BigDataPE
code: all_data |>   count(st_ocorreu_hospitalizacao) |>   mutate(description = case_match(     st_ocorreu_hospitalizacao,     "1" ~ "Yes",     "2" ~ "No",     "9" ~ "Unknown",     ""  ~ "Not reported"   )) #> # A tibble: 4 x 3 #>   st_ocorreu_hospitalizacao     n description #>   <chr>                     <int> <chr> #> 1                             330 Not reported #> 2 1                            51 Yes #> 3 2                           565 No #> 4 9                            63 Unknown

title: Exploring Dengue Data with BigDataPE
code: my_token <- bdpe_get_token("dengue")

title: Exploring Dengue Data with BigDataPE
text: end session, token longer needed:
code: bdpe_remove_token("dengue") #> ✔ Token successfully removed for dataset: "dengue"

what: Secure and Intuitive Access to BigDataPE API Datasets
title: Secure and Intuitive Access to BigDataPE API Datasets
text: BigDataPE R package provides secure intuitive way access datasets BigDataPE platform. package allows users fetch data API using token-based authentication, manage multiple tokens different datasets, retrieve data efficiently using chunking.

title: Secure and Intuitive Access to BigDataPE API Datasets
text: can install BigDataPE package directly GitHub: installation, load package:
code: # Install the devtools package if you haven't already install.packages("devtools")  # Install BigDataPE from GitHub devtools::install_github("StrategicProjects/bigdatape") library(BigDataPE)

title: Secure and Intuitive Access to BigDataPE API Datasets
text: Securely store manage API tokens environment variables. Fetch data BigDataPE API using simple interface. Retrieve large datasets iteratively using chunking. Easily manage multiple datasets associated tokens.

title: Secure and Intuitive Access to BigDataPE API Datasets
text: function securely stores authentication token specific dataset. Parameters: base_name: name dataset. token: authentication token dataset. Example:
code: bdpe_store_token(base_name, token) bdpe_store_token("education_dataset", "your-token-here")

title: Secure and Intuitive Access to BigDataPE API Datasets
text: function retrieves securely stored token specific dataset. Parameters: base_name: name dataset. Example:
code: bdpe_get_token(base_name) token <- bdpe_get_token("education_dataset")

title: Secure and Intuitive Access to BigDataPE API Datasets
text: function removes token associated specific dataset. Parameters: base_name: name dataset. Example:
code: bdpe_remove_token(base_name) bdpe_remove_token("education_dataset")

title: Secure and Intuitive Access to BigDataPE API Datasets
text: function lists datasets stored tokens. Example:
code: bdpe_list_tokens() datasets <- bdpe_list_tokens() print(datasets)

title: Secure and Intuitive Access to BigDataPE API Datasets
text: function retrieves data BigDataPE API using securely stored tokens. Parameters: base_name: name dataset. limit: Number records per page. Default Inf offset: Starting record query. Default 0. query: Additional query parameters. endpoint: API endpoint URL. Example:
code: bdpe_fetch_data(   base_name,    limit = 100,    offset = 0,    query = list(),    endpoint = "https://www.bigdata.pe.gov.br/api/buscar") data <- bdpe_fetch_data("education_dataset", limit = 50)

title: Secure and Intuitive Access to BigDataPE API Datasets
text: function retrieves data API iteratively chunks. Parameters: base_name: name dataset. total_limit: Maximum number records fetch. Default Inf (fetch available data). chunk_size: Number records per chunk. Default 50.000 query: Additional query parameters. endpoint: API endpoint URL. Example:
code: bdpe_fetch_chunks(   base_name,    total_limit = Inf,    chunk_size = 100,    query = list(),    endpoint = "https://www.bigdata.pe.gov.br/api/buscar") # Fetch up to 500 records in chunks of 100 data <- bdpe_fetch_chunks(           "education_dataset",            total_limit = 500,            chunk_size = 100)  # Fetch all available data in chunks of 200 all_data <- bdpe_fetch_chunks(               "education_dataset",                chunk_size = 200)

what: 7. Construct URL with Query Parameters: parse_queries
title: Secure and Intuitive Access to BigDataPE API Datasets
text: internal function constructs URL query parameters. Parameters: url: base URL. query_list: list query parameters. Example:
code: parse_queries(url, query_list) url <- parse_queries(             "https://www.example.com",              list(param1 = "value1", param2 = "value2")             ) print(url)

title: Secure and Intuitive Access to BigDataPE API Datasets
text: ’s complete example workflow:
code: # Store a token for a dataset bdpe_store_token("education_dataset", "your-token-here")  # Fetch 100 records starting from the first record data <- bdpe_fetch_data("education_dataset", limit = 100, offset = 0)  # Fetch data in chunks all_data <- bdpe_fetch_chunks(   "education_dataset",    total_limit = 500,    chunk_size = 100)  # List all datasets with stored tokens datasets <- bdpe_list_tokens()  # Remove a token bdpe_remove_token("education_dataset")

title: Secure and Intuitive Access to BigDataPE API Datasets

what: Fetch data from the BigDataPE API in chunks — bdpe_fetch_chunks
title: Fetch data from the BigDataPE API in chunks — bdpe_fetch_chunks
text: function retrieves data BigDataPE API iteratively chunks. uses bdpe_fetch_data base function supports limits total number records fetch size chunk.

title: Fetch data from the BigDataPE API in chunks — bdpe_fetch_chunks
code: bdpe_fetch_chunks(   base_name,   total_limit = Inf,   chunk_size = 500000L,   query = list(),   verbosity = 0L,   endpoint = "https://www.bigdata.pe.gov.br/api/buscar" )

title: Fetch data from the BigDataPE API in chunks — bdpe_fetch_chunks
text: base_name string specifying name dataset associated token. total_limit integer specifying maximum number records fetch. Default Inf (available data). chunk_size integer specifying number records fetch per chunk. Default 50000 query named list additional query parameters filter API results. Default empty list. verbosity integer specifying verbosity level. Values : - 0: output (default). - 1: Show progress messages (records fetched, totals). - 2: Show progress messages full HTTP request/response details. endpoint string specifying API endpoint URL. Default "https://www.bigdata.pe.gov.br/api/buscar".

title: Fetch data from the BigDataPE API in chunks — bdpe_fetch_chunks
text: tibble containing data retrieved API.

title: Fetch data from the BigDataPE API in chunks — bdpe_fetch_chunks
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Store a token for the dataset bdpe_store_token("dengue_dataset", "token")  # Fetch up to 500 records in chunks of 100 data <- bdpe_fetch_chunks("dengue_dataset", total_limit = 500, chunk_size = 100)  # Fetch all available data in chunks of 200 data <- bdpe_fetch_chunks("dengue_dataset", chunk_size = 200) } # }

what: Fetch data from the BigDataPE API — bdpe_fetch_data
title: Fetch data from the BigDataPE API — bdpe_fetch_data
text: function retrieves data BigDataPE API using securely stored tokens associated datasets. Users can specify pagination parameters (limit offset) additional query filters customize data retrieval.

title: Fetch data from the BigDataPE API — bdpe_fetch_data
code: bdpe_fetch_data(   base_name,   limit = Inf,   offset = 0L,   query = list(),   verbosity = 0L,   endpoint = "https://www.bigdata.pe.gov.br/api/buscar" )

title: Fetch data from the BigDataPE API — bdpe_fetch_data
text: base_name string specifying name dataset associated token. limit integer specifying maximum number records retrieve per request. Default Inf (records). set non-positive value Inf, limit applied. offset integer specifying starting record query. Default 0. set non-positive value Inf, offset applied. query named list additional query parameters filter API results. Default empty list. verbosity integer specifying verbosity level. Values : - 0: output (default). - 1: Show progress messages (records fetched, totals). - 2: Show progress messages full HTTP request/response details. endpoint string specifying API endpoint URL. Default "https://www.bigdata.pe.gov.br/api/buscar".

title: Fetch data from the BigDataPE API — bdpe_fetch_data
text: tibble containing data returned API.

title: Fetch data from the BigDataPE API — bdpe_fetch_data
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Store a token for the dataset bdpe_store_token("dengue_dataset", "token")  # Fetch 50 records from the beginning data <- bdpe_fetch_data("dengue_dataset", limit = 50)  # Fetch records with additional query parameters data <- bdpe_fetch_data("dengue_dataset", query = list(field = "value"))  # Fetch all data without limits data <- bdpe_fetch_data("dengue_dataset", limit = Inf) } # }

what: Store a token in an environment variable for a specific dataset — bdpe_store_token
title: Store a token in an environment variable for a specific dataset — bdpe_store_token
text: function stores authentication token specific dataset system environment variable. environment variable name constructed converting dataset name ASCII (removing accents), replacing spaces underscores, prefixing "BigDataPE_".

code: bdpe_store_token(base_name, token)

text: base_name name dataset (character). token authentication token dataset (character).

text: variable name already exists (non-empty), function stop notify avoid overwriting. Adjust behavior needed.

title: Store a token in an environment variable for a specific dataset — bdpe_store_token
code: bdpe_store_token("education_dataset", "your-token-here") #> ✔ Token stored in environment variable: `BigDataPE_education_dataset`

title: Constructs a URL with query parameters — parse_queries

text: url base URL query parameters added. query_list list query parameters added URL.

title: Constructs a URL with query parameters — parse_queries
code: parse_queries("https://www.example.com", list(param1 = "value1", param2 = "value2")) #> [1] "https://www.example.com?param1=value1&param2=value2"

title: BigDataPE 0.1.0
text: Migrated user-facing messages, warnings, errors base R (message(), stop(), stopifnot()) cli package (cli::cli_abort(), cli::cli_alert_success(), cli::cli_alert_warning(), cli::cli_alert_info()). provides richer, informative, consistently formatted console output. Added progress feedback bdpe_fetch_chunks() verbosity > 0, showing number records fetched per chunk running total. Added completion summary message bdpe_fetch_chunks() reporting total number records retrieved. Improved input validation bdpe_fetch_data() bdpe_fetch_chunks() descriptive cli error messages replacing stopifnot() calls. Numeric parameters (limit, offset, total_limit, chunk_size, verbosity) now automatically coerced integer possible (e.g., limit = 50 now works without requiring limit = 50L). informative error raised conversion possible (e.g., non-numeric fractional values). Improved HTTP error handling bdpe_fetch_data(): connection failures HTTP errors (e.g., 503 Service Unavailable) now produce clear, user-friendly messages guidance network requirements next steps, instead raw httr2 errors.

title: BigDataPE 0.1.0
text: Added cli package dependency Imports. Added Marcos Wasilew Carlos Amorin package authors. Added vignette “Exploring Dengue Data BigDataPE” demonstrating full package workflow public dengue dataset.

title: BigDataPE 0.0.96
text: CRAN release: 2025-04-02

title: BigDataPE 0.0.96
text: Fixed issue bdpe_fetch_chunks() offset parameter formatted scientific double (e.g., 1e+05), caused API fail. value now properly formatted integer string, ensuring compatibility API expects exact integer format.
